sanskrit:
भक्त्या मामभिजानाति यावान्यश्चास्मि तत्त्वतः।
ततो मां तत्त्वतो ज्ञात्वा विशते तदनन्तरम्॥
transliteration:
bhaktyā mām abhijānāti yāvān yaś cāsmi tattvataḥ |
tato māṃ tattvato jñātvā viśate tad-anantaram ||
literal_meaning: By devotion one knows Me as I truly am—how great and who I am; knowing Me thus in truth, one immediately enters Me.
contextual_meaning: Only through loving, participatory knowing (bhakti) can the mind grasp the full depth of Being; once this lived recognition occurs, the sense of separation dissolves and one 'enters' a field of intimate belonging.
psychological_state: Cognitive surrender—mind abandons the attempt to objectify the Absolute and instead allows itself to be known by the Other through sustained relational practice.
trigger_scenario: A high-achieving professional realizes that no amount of data or credentials explains why their child loves them; only when they drop the expert stance and simply receive the child’s hug do they ‘know’ and ‘enter’ the relationship.
anchor_text: Love reveals who I am; knowing Me, you merge into Me.
